samples.xml_description:
<module id="div_16bit">
    <description>Design a simplified radix-2 divider on 8-bit numbers.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst">Reset signal.</input>
        <input id="sign">Signed or unsigned operation.</input>
        <input id="dividend" width_description="[15:0]">Input operand DIVIDEND.</input>
        <input id="divisor" width_description="[15:0]">Input operand DIVISOR.</input>
        <input id="opn_valid">Valid operation request.</input>
        <input id="res_ready">The output has been acknowledged.</input>
        <output id="res_valid">The result is valid.</output>
        <output id="result" width_description="[15:0]">Output result.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <param id="IDLE">0</param>
        <param id="START">1</param>
        <param id="SUB">2</param>
        <param id="UPDATE_SR">3</param>
        <param id="DONE">4</param>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <sequential_logic id="div_valid">
            <assign>
                <at> posedge clk </at>
                <if>
                    <true>
                        <assign> div_valid = opn_valid & ~res_valid; </assign>
                    </true>
                </if>
            </assign>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="cnt">
            <assign>
                <at> posedge clk </at>
                <if>
                    <true>
                        <assign> cnt = div_valid ? 4'b0001 : cnt; </assign>
                    </true>
                    <false>
                        <assign> cnt = div_valid ? 4'b0001 : (cnt[3] ? 4'b0 : cnt + 4'b0001); </assign>
                    </false>
                </if>
            </assign>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="start_cnt">
            <assign>
                <at> posedge clk </at>
                <if>
                    <true>
                        <assign> start_cnt = div_valid; </assign>
                    </true>
                    <false>
                        <assign> start_cnt = cnt[3]; </assign>
                    </false>
                </if>
            </assign>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="res_valid">
            <assign>
                <at> posedge clk </at>
                <if>
                    <true>
                        <assign> res_valid = rst ? 1'b0 : (cnt[3] ? 1'b1 : (res_valid & res_ready ? 1'b0 : res_valid)); </assign>
                    </true>
                </if>
            </assign>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="dividend_reg">
            <assign>
                <at> posedge clk </at>
                <if>
                    <true>
                        <assign> dividend_reg = div_valid ? (sign & dividend[15] ? ~dividend + 1'b1 : dividend) : (cnt[3] ? (sign & dividend_reg[15] ? ~dividend_reg + 1'b1 : dividend_reg) : dividend_reg); </assign>
                    </true>
                </if>
            </assign>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="divisor_reg">
            <assign>
                <at> posedge clk </at>
                <if>
                    <true>
                        <assign> divisor_reg = div_valid ? (sign & divisor[15] ? (~divisor + 1'b1) : divisor) : (cnt[3] ? divisor_reg : 16'bx); </assign>
                    </true>
                </if>
            </assign>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="neg_divisor">
            <assign>
                <at> posedge clk </at>
                <if>
                    <true>
                        <assign> neg_divisor = div_valid ? (sign & divisor[15] ?  (divisor) : ~divisor + 1'b1) : (cnt[3] ? divisor_reg : 16'bx); </assign>
                    </true>
                </if>
            </assign>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="shift_reg">
            <assign>
                <at> posedge clk </at>
                <if>
                    <true>
                        <assign> shift_reg = div_valid ? (sign & dividend[15] ? {dividend[15], dividend, 1'b0} : {dividend[15], dividend, 1'b0}) : (cnt[3] ? (shift_reg[15] ? {1'b1, shift_reg[15:1]} : {1'b0, shift_reg[15:1]}) : shift_reg); </assign>
                    </true>
                    <false>
                        <assign> shift_reg = div_valid ? (sign & dividend[15] ? {dividend[15], dividend, 1'b0} : {dividend[15], dividend, 1'b0}) : (cnt[3] ? (shift_reg[15] ? (sign & divisor[15] ? {shift_reg[15], shift_reg[15:1]} : {1'b1, shift_reg[15:1]}) : shift_reg) : shift_reg); </assign>
                    </false>
                </if>
            </assign>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="result">
            <assign>
                <at> posedge clk </at>
                <if>
                    <true>
                        <assign> result = cnt[3] ? (sign & dividend[15] ^ divisor[15] ? ~shift_reg + 1'b1 : shift_reg) : 16'bx; </assign>
                    </true>
                </if>
            </assign>
        </sequential_logic>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
    The module accepts two 8-bit inputs, DIVIDEND and DIVISOR, and provides a 16-bit output, RESULT, containing the quotient and remainder.
    The module starts when the input valid signal (opn_valid) is high and the output valid signal (res_valid) is low.
    The initial shift register (SR) is set to the absolute value of the dividend shifted left by one bit.
    The negative divisor (NEG_DIVISOR) is set to the negated absolute value of the divisor.
    The counter (cnt) is set to 1, and the start control signal (start_cnt) is set to 1 to initiate the division process.
    If the counter reaches 8 (most significant bit of cnt is set), the division is complete, and the module clears cnt and start_cnt.
    Otherwise, the counter is incremented, and the shift register is updated based on the subtraction result.
    The subtraction is performed by adding the two's complement of the divisor.
    The multiplexer selects the result based on the carry-out from the subtraction.
    The shift register is updated by shifting left and inserting the carry-out.
    The output valid signal (res_valid) is set to 1 when cnt reaches 8 and the operation is complete.
    If the output valid signal is high and the result is ready, the output valid signal is cleared.
    </implementation>
</module>
